def sieve(max)
  is_prime = Array.new(max + 1, true)
  is_prime[0] = is_prime[1] = false
  (2..Math.sqrt(max)).each do |i|
    if is_prime[i]
      (i*i..max).step(i) { |j| is_prime[j] = false }
    end
  end
  is_prime
end

def solve(nums)
  n = nums.length
  if n == 0
    return 0
  end

  # Precompute sieve up to max value in nums
  max_val = nums.max
  sieve = Array.new(max_val + 1, true)
  sieve[0] = sieve[1] = false
  (2..Math.sqrt(max_val)).each do |i|
    if sieve[i]
      (i*i..max_val).step(i) { |j| sieve[j] = false }
    end
  end

  # Preprocess value_indices
  value_indices = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
  nums.each_with_index do |v, i|
    value_indices[v] << i
  end

  # BFS
  visited = Array.new(n, false)
  queue = [[0, 0]]
  visited[0] = true

  while queue.any?
    current, steps = queue.shift
    if current == n - 1
      return steps
    end

    # Check adjacent indices
    [current - 1, current + 1].each do |next_idx|
      if next_idx >= 0 && next_idx < n && !visited[next_idx]
        visited[next_idx] = true
        queue << [next_idx, steps + 1]
      end
    end

    # Check if current is a prime
    current_val = nums[current]
    if sieve[current_val]
      # Find all multiples of current_val
      p = current_val
      (p..max_val).step(p) do |m|
        if value_indices.key?(m)
          value_indices[m].each do |j|
            if j != current && !visited[j]
              visited[j] = true
              queue << [j, steps + 1]
            end
          end
        end
      end
    end
  end

  # If not found (should not happen)
  -1
end

```ruby
def solve(nums)
  n = nums.length
  return 0 if n == 0

  max_num = 1_000_000
  sieve = Array.new(max_num + 1, true)
  sieve[0] = false
  sieve[1] = false
  (2..Math.sqrt(max_num)).each do |i|
    if sieve[i]
      (i*i..max_num).step(i) { |j| sieve[j] = false }
    end
  end

  prime_indices = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
  nums.each_with_index do |x, j|
    factors = get_prime_factors(x)
    factors.each do |p|
      prime_indices[p] << j
    end
  end

  visited = Array.new(n, -1)
  queue = [0]
  visited[0] = 0

  while !queue.empty?
    current = queue.shift
    if current == n - 1
      return visited[current]
    end

    [current - 1, current + 1].each do |next_i|
      if next_i >= 0 && next_i < n && visited[next_i] == -1
        visited[next_i] = visited[current] + 1
        queue << next_i
      end
    end

    x = nums[current]
    if sieve[x]
      indices = prime_indices[x]
      indices.each do |j|
        if j != current && visited[j] == -1
          visited[j] = visited[current] + 1
          queue << j
        end
      end
    end
  end

  -1
end

def get_prime_factors(x)
  factors = []
  if x % 2 == 0
    factors << 2
    while x % 2 == 0
      x /= 2
    end
  end
  i = 3
  while i*i <= x
    if x % i == 0
      factors << i
      while x % i == 0
        x /= i
      end
    end
    i += 2
  end
  if x > 1
    factors << x
  end
  factors.uniq
end
```

def solve(nums)
    n = nums.length
    max_num = nums.max
    # Precompute SPF
    spf = Array.new(max_num + 1, 0)
    (2..max_num).each do |i|
        if spf[i] == 0
            spf[i] = i
            (i*2..max_num).step(i) do |j|
                if spf[j] == 0
                    spf[j] = i
                end
            end
        end
    end

    # Now, for each number in nums, find its prime factors
    prime_map = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
    # For each index i, find primes that divide nums[i]
    nums.each_with_index do |x, i|
        # Find all primes that divide x
        factors = Set.new
        if x == 1
            next
        end
        temp = x
        while temp > 1
            p = spf[temp]
            factors.add(p)
            while temp % p == 0
                temp /= p
            end
        end
        # Now, factors contains all primes that divide x
        # For each prime in factors, add i to the map
        factors.each do |p|
            prime_map[p] << i
        end
    end

    # Now, BFS
    visited = Array.new(n, false)
    queue = Queue.new
    queue.enq([0, 0]) # [index, steps]
    visited[0] = true

    while !queue.empty?
        idx, steps = queue.deq
        if idx == n-1
            return steps
        end
        # Check adjacent indices
        [idx-1, idx+1].each do |next_idx|
            if next_idx >=0 && next_idx < n && !visited[next_idx]
                visited[next_idx] = true
                queue.enq([next_idx, steps + 1])
            end
        end
        # Check if current number is a prime
        current = nums[idx]
        if current > 1
            if spf[current] == current
                # current is a prime
                primes = prime_map[current]
                primes.each do |j|
                    if j != idx
                        if !visited[j]
                            visited[j] = true
                            queue.enq([j, steps + 1])
                        end
                    end
                end
            end
        end
    end
    # If not found (should not happen)
    return -1
end
